treatment: RSL3 (pharmacologic; immune/stromal/pH off to isolate the pharmacologic kill)

supply_gradient_direction.verdict: bigger-spheroids-resist-more direction reproduced and fold-drop in the measured cytotoxic range (DIRECTION + order of magnitude validated; magnitude uncalibrated)

persister_targeting_prediction.prediction: RSL3 preferentially kills the persister phenotype, and the size-aware persister core only emerges above ~280 µm, so small all-proliferating spheroids RESIST RSL3 (no persister target) and vulnerability emerges with the core, the OPPOSITE size-direction to generic cytotoxics. Falsifiable; untestable without ferroptosis-inducer size-kill data.

cytotoxic_comparator_sources: West 1989 Br J Cancer (WiDr spheroids, PDT): ~1.8x/2.5x/22x less sensitive at 100/250/500 µm
Eilenberger 2021 Adv Sci (A549, doxorubicin/cisplatin): 2.3 to 6.9x IC50 increase large vs small
Demuynck 2020 PMID 32183000 (3DELTA, ML-162 ferroptosis): ~50% to ~30% kill small to large (2-point)
scope: Kill MAGNITUDE uncalibrated (in-vivo-resistant switch + placeholder gradients). The cytotoxic data validates the supply-gradient DIRECTION only (wrong drug class for a persister-targeting inducer). The persister-targeting prediction is falsifiable but data-blocked. STRUCTURE was validated separately (#333 structure leg + size-aware).
